sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.98 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$F$be a free group of finite rank,$R$its recursively defined normal sub\-group. Is it true that the word problem for$F/R$is soluble if and only if it is soluble for$F/[R,R]$? the conjugacy problem for$F/R$is soluble if and only if it is soluble for$F/[R,R]$? the conjugacy problem for$F/[R,R]$is soluble if the word problem is soluble for$F/[R,R]$? { V.\,E.\,Shpil'rain} a) Yes, it is; b) No, it i...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
